strategy_decisions.rationale: This BTC value-recovery scale-in concept never produced a valid-data trade. Even with a broader reclaim entry and conditional add-on, the mechanism still required too much sequential confirmation to participate on the lane, so another rescue attempt would just cosmetically loosen a dead grammar.

The ETH persistence continuation with asymmetric exits also hard-skipped with zero trades. Role separation improved conceptual cleanliness, but it did not solve the core problem that the entry still failed to engage often enough for honest testing.

This TAO retest-hold continuation also produced zero valid-data trades. Time-based de-risking never had a chance to matter because the retest-hold grammar itself remained structurally sparse, so it should be abandoned rather than diluted further.
